[30;1m3561 | [0m[35m[0m
[30;1m3562 | [0m[35m// Validates as many continuation bytes for a multi-byte UTF-8 character as[0m
[30;1m3563 | [0m[35m// needed or are available. If we see a non-continuation byte where we expect[0m
[30;1m3564 | [0m[35m// one, we "replace" the validated continuation bytes we've seen so far with[0m
[30;1m3565 | [0m[35m// UTF-8 replacement characters ('\ufffd'), to match v8's UTF-8 decoding[0m
[30;1m3566 | [0m[35m// behavior. The continuation byte check is included three times in the case[0m
[30;1m3567 | [0m[35m// where all of the continuation bytes for a character exist in the same buffer.[0m

[30;1m31 | [0m[35m[0m
[30;1m32 | [0m[35mNow the `<List />` component will always reflect the values you have stored in your database with `p[0m
[30;1m33 | [0m[35m[0m
[30;1m34 | [0m[35mFind a full example in [/example](example):[0m
[30;1m35 | [0m[35m[0m
[30;1m36 | [0m[35m```bash[0m
[30;1m37 | [0m[35m$ npm install[0m

256 matches found.
